summarize.prompt:
Summarize this in 3-5 concise bullet points. Lead with the most important:

{text}

action-items.prompt:
Extract every actionable item from this text. Number them. Be specific — include who, what, when if present.

{text}

explain-code.prompt:
Explain this code in plain language. What does it do? What's the intent? Any concerns?

```
{text}
```

review-code.prompt:
Review this code for bugs, security issues, and improvements. Be specific and actionable.

```
{text}
```

audit.prompt:
Perform a comprehensive audit of this project area. Cover: working well, issues, technical debt, and recommended next steps.

{text}

breakdown.description: Break down a feature into tasks
breakdown.prompt:
Break this feature down into specific, actionable tasks. Estimate complexity for each.

{text}

compare.prompt:
Compare these two approaches. Cover pros, cons, and when to use each.

{text}

brainstorm.prompt:
Generate creative solutions and ideas for this problem. Think broadly.

{text}

test-plan.description: Generate test plan for a feature
test-plan.prompt:
Create a test plan for this feature. Cover: unit tests, integration tests, edge cases, and success criteria.

{text}

migrate-plan.prompt:
Create a migration plan for this change. Cover: steps, risks, rollback plan, and verification.

{text}

architect.prompt:
Create an architecture decision record. Cover: problem, options considered, decision, and consequences.

{text}

canadian-check.prompt:
Rewrite this explanation or advice using strict Canadian context. Convert temperatures to Celsius, distances to kilometres, and use analogies involving Canadian Tire, winter driving, or hockey if applicable. Keep it dry and practical.

{text}
